title: rubus quadicus
side_effects: Rubus quadicus can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea and stomach cramps, due to its high tannin content. In excessive doses, it may also cause allergic reactions, such as hives and itching, as well as interact with certain medications. Furthermore, improper use of Rubus quadicus can lead to kidney damage and increased blood pressure in susceptible individuals. In severe cases, consuming large amounts of the plant can cause dizziness, headaches, and even seizures.
